title: Chimichurri
cScaleSummary: C0 because the recipe contains no cholesterol-contributing animal-derived ingredients. The parsley, oregano, garlic, shallot, vinegar, lemon juice, spices, and olive oil contribute 0 mg cholesterol.
ldlActiveSummary: LDL-Active because the sauce is plant-based, uses extra-virgin olive oil as the fat source, and builds flavor through fresh herbs, garlic, vinegar, lemon, and spices rather than saturated fat or cholesterol-containing ingredients.
intro: Argentine chimichurri is a sharp, herb-heavy sauce built for grilled meats, but it works just as well on roasted vegetables, beans, potatoes, grilled mushrooms, chicken, fish, or as a punchy sandwich spread. It should taste bright, garlicky, grassy, and a little aggressive. It is not pesto. Keep it loose, chopped, and vinegar-forward.

stepGroups.title: Make the herb base
items: Finely chop 1 cup fresh flat-leaf parsley and 2 tablespoons fresh oregano. Finely mince 3 cloves garlic and 1 small shallot. Add everything to a bowl.

stepGroups.title: Season and acidify
items: Add 3 tablespoons red wine vinegar, 1 tablespoon fresh lemon juice, 1 teaspoon kosher salt, 0.5 teaspoon black pepper, 0.5 teaspoon crushed red pepper flakes, and 0.5 teaspoon smoked paprika. Stir well and let the mixture sit for 10 minutes so the garlic and shallot soften in the vinegar.

stepGroups.title: Finish with oil
items: Stir in 0.5 cup extra-virgin olive oil. Do not blend it. Chimichurri should stay loose and rustic, not creamy.

stepGroups.title: Rest before serving
items: Let the chimichurri sit at least 30 minutes before serving. Taste and adjust only with salt or vinegar. It should be sharper than expected when tasted by itself, because grilled food will tame it.

stepGroups.title: Best uses
items: Serve with grilled chicken, grilled steak, roasted potatoes, grilled mushrooms, white beans, rice bowls, roasted cauliflower, sandwiches, eggs, or shrimp.
